compositional_truth_check.findings: P3 (Pothos/Rabbit) and P1 (String of Nickels) are present. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not clearly identifiable as distinct, healthy specimens; the soil in the black pot (P3/P4 zone) shows signs of significant decay or lack of established foliage.

systemic_losses: P2 (Mexican Mint): No visible healthy foliage.
P4 (Silver Guest): No visible healthy foliage.
new_introductions: Eggshell fragments observed in P3/P4 soil as a soil amendment/calcium supplement.

plant_audit.P1_String_of_Nickels: Stable, minimal growth, leaf turgor appears consistent.
plant_audit.P3_Pothos: Primary specimen remains with the rabbit anchor; leaf health is stagnant but stable.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Combined_Pot: Severe systemic loss; only a single, pale, possibly etiolated sprout remains. High probability of root rot or nutrient deficiency.

biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in the lower black pot, likely an intentional intervention.
biome_observations.surface_conditions: No fungal blooms detected, but soil appears overly saturated.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The primary change is the gradual loss of secondary foliage in the black pot and the introduction of eggshell debris. The Pothos leaf in the rabbit pot has maintained its position, while the sprout in the lower pot has shown no significant expansion or maturation.

anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments suggests an attempt to alter soil pH or calcium levels.
Lack of visible Mexican Mint (P2) suggests total loss.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of stasis with signs of decline in the secondary specimens. The Pothos (P3) and String of Nickels (P1) are the only surviving robust elements. The intervention of eggshells indicates an attempt to rescue the soil environment, but the lack of new growth in the lower pot suggests the root systems may be compromised. The environment is stable but requires immediate intervention to prevent further loss.
